behaviors.description: The evaluate-stage command stores grounding into a dedicated `grounding` artifact, not the `plan` artifact.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is command-to-artifact-store handoff (module interaction), not pure local algorithm. REAL-INFRA: no OS/network/subprocess required. LLM-DEP: behavior is about artifact key/slot selection, not model output quality. Rejection-gate: rejected Agentic because behavior text is storage routing ('stores grounding into...artifact'), not correctness of LLM-generated grounding.
behaviors.test_description: Run evaluate-stage with an in-process artifact repository and a fixed grounding payload; assert `grounding` artifact is created/updated and `plan` is untouched by this write path.

behaviors.description: The implement-stage flow reads grounding when available and still writes its execution output to `plan`.
behaviors.justification: This is cross-module orchestration (read one artifact, write another) within local process wiring. REAL-INFRA: no real OS/network/subprocess needed. LLM-DEP: assertions target dataflow destinations, not semantic quality of generated text. Rejection-gate: rejected Agentic because behavior text focuses on read/write contract ('reads grounding...writes...to plan'), which a deterministic stub can validate.
behaviors.test_description: Seed run context with a `grounding` artifact, execute implement-stage with deterministic generator stub, and assert grounding is read (observable via input to implement logic) while final write target is `plan` only.

behaviors.description: Deep-dive no longer overwrites `plan` as a redundant side effect when diagnosis already exists in issue content.
behaviors.justification: Bug appears at boundary between deep-dive stage control logic and artifact persistence side effects. REAL-INFRA: can be reproduced with in-memory issue content + artifact store. LLM-DEP: requirement is side-effect suppression condition, not model judgment quality. Rejection-gate: rejected Workflow because behavior text is a single-stage side effect rule ('Deep-dive no longer overwrites `plan`').
behaviors.test_description: Provide issue content that already includes diagnosis and a preexisting `plan` artifact; run deep-dive stage and assert no write/mutation occurs to `plan`.

behaviors.description: Artifact lineage is preserved so implement can read the `grounding` artifact produced earlier in the same run context.
behaviors.justification: Failure is state handoff across local modules/stages in shared run context. REAL-INFRA: no requirement for real network/OS primitives if lineage store is local/in-process abstraction. LLM-DEP: correctness here is lineage availability/readability, not model content quality. Rejection-gate: rejected Workflow because behavior text does not require validating multiple model decisions, only persistence/handoff continuity.
behaviors.test_description: Within one run context, execute evaluate-stage write of `grounding`, then invoke implement-stage and assert it resolves the same artifact lineage entry (same run-scoped identifier/version chain).

behaviors.description: In a full multi-step agent run (`deep-dive -> write-plan/evaluate -> implement`), the final implementation plan reflects grounding produced earlier in the run.
behaviors.justification: PROOF-BUNDLE (1) Forcing phrase: 'full multi-step agent run (deep-dive -> write-plan/evaluate -> implement)' plus 'final implementation plan reflects grounding produced earlier'. (2) Adjacent-lower miss (Agentic): a single-step agentic test can show implement responds to grounding in isolation, but misses failures where earlier stage output is not propagated/retained correctly across the full chain; this is specifically sequential handoff across multiple agentic stages, not generic 'wiring could fail'. SELF-CHECK: test must execute the full chain with real model decisions at multiple points, so lower levels are insufficient.
behaviors.test_description: Run an end-to-end chain in one real run context with grounding signal introduced upstream; assert final `plan` content materially incorporates that grounding signal and that this dependence survives all intermediate stage transitions.

behaviors.description: When grounding is absent, implement falls back safely and still emits a valid plan artifact without corrupting other stored artifacts.
behaviors.justification: This is fallback-path orchestration and artifact integrity across modules. REAL-INFRA: no real OS/network/subprocess required. LLM-DEP: core assertion is safe fallback + artifact non-corruption; can be validated with deterministic output fixture. Rejection-gate: rejected Agentic because behavior text does not require judging model quality, only safe behavior when 'grounding is absent'.
behaviors.test_description: Execute implement-stage in a run context lacking `grounding`; assert `plan` is emitted in expected schema and preexisting unrelated artifacts remain byte-for-byte unchanged.
